commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds pond grouping with `--pond`/`--expose`, cross-provider peer discovery, URL bridge adapters, SSH-mesh connect/release commands, Worker label propagation, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of the PR head shows the narrow HuJSON normalizer, pending transport omission, and raw provider comparison. I did not execute the PR branch because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A repair lane can address the three concrete code blockers, while maintainers still decide the preview/security boundary separately.

target.title: feat: introduce pond (peer discovery + bridge + ssh-mesh)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `--pond NAME` and reserved `pond=` provider label for workflows that need more than one box talking to each other on a private network: multi-service integration tests where compose's loopback hides cross-host bugs (saga / mTLS / CORS / federation timing), CPU + GPU pipelines across providers because no single vendor stocks both at the same price point, cross-repo CI, master/worker compute (BOINC / Dask / Ray cluster shape — not tightly-coupled MPI / NCCL all-reduce, which the cross-vendor RTT can't carry). Single-box flows unchanged ("pond of one").
- Three transport planes, derived from each provider's `Spec().Features` set (no central hardcoded mapping):
  - Tailscale (`FeatureTailscale`) — managed Linux peer mesh via ACL tag + cloud-init systemd timer rewriting `/etc/hosts.cbx`; stable `<slug>.cbx` DNS.
  - Bridge (`FeatureURLBridge`) — Islo / E2B / Railway live; Modal / Cloudflare / Tensorlake stubs return `BridgeState=unsupported` until provider APIs expose per-sandbox ingress.
  - SSH-mesh (`FeatureSSH`) — operator-side `ssh -L` via `crabbox pond connect [--export]`. Works against every provider that advertises FeatureSSH: Hetzner / Azure / GCP / AWS / Proxmox / static SSH / RunPod / exe-dev / Daytona / Sprites / Namespace / Semaphore / local-container / Parallels.
- `BridgePeer.transports[]` reports the full plane set per member; legacy `transport` field still reports the primary (Tailscale > URL > SSH-mesh).
- New verbs: `crabbox pond peers --pond <name>`, `crabbox pond connect <name> [--export]`. `crabbox doctor --pond <name>` reports cross-plane reachability.
- Worker reserves the `pond` label key.
- Renamed `crew` → `pond` (the org uses crab-biology naming; `pool` was taken by `internal/cli/pool.go`).
- Folds #136 (bridge plane) and #137 (ssh-mesh) into this PR.

## Verification

- `go test -race ./internal/cli/...` — 47 tests green, ~41s
- `go vet ./...`
- `( cd worker && pnpm test && pnpm typecheck )`
- `crabbox doctor --pond smoke` against a real Hetzner tailnet

## Live smoke — Bridge plane (Islo)

```
$ crabbox warmup --pond ps001 --slug w --provider islo --islo-memory-mb 2048 --islo-disk-gb 10 --ttl 5m
leased isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e slug=w provider=islo sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
warmup complete total=42.17s

$ crabbox pond peers --pond ps001 --json
{"members":[{"slug":"w","leaseID":"isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e","provider":"islo","pond":"ps001","transport":"url","transports":["url"],"endpoint":""}]}

$ crabbox release --provider islo isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
released lease=isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
```

## Live smoke — Tailscale plane (ACL bootstrap end-to-end)

Against a real tailnet with `TS_API_KEY` exported. Read live policy (HuJSON-aware decode — R1 fix), merged the new row into `tagOwners` + `grants`, PUT with `If-Match`.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider hetzner --pond live-smoke --slug node-1 --tailscale --ttl 1m
# (warmup fails on the missing CRABBOX_TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY check; ACL write fires *before* provider provisioning.)

$ curl -s -u $TS_API_KEY: https://api.tailscale.com/api/v2/tailnet/-/acl | jq '.tagOwners, (.grants[] | select(.src[0] | startswith("tag:cbx-pond-")))'
{ "tag:cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha": ["autogroup:admin"],
  "tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke": ["autogroup:admin"] }
{ "src": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "dst": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "ip":  ["*"] }
```

New owner segment is `ipdkyao` — `base32(sha256("yossi-eliaz"))[:7]` (R4 fix) — sitting beside the stale `cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha` row from earlier testing, confirming the hash does not collide. Idempotent re-run of `crabbox doctor --pond live-smoke --provider hetzner` reports `ok pond: Tailscale plane auto-managed (tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke)`. Test row cleaned up: `grep -c cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke` on the live policy = 0.

## Live smoke — SSH-mesh cross-provider (local-container / Docker)

Proves the capability refactor's load-bearing claim: a non-SSH-only provider (`local-container` advertises `FeatureSSH` but is not in the legacy SSH-only class) is now `pond connect`-eligible.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider local-container --pond local-smoke --slug web --expose 8080 --ttl 5m
provisioned lease=cbx_3960ccf8b93a container=aeb808878095 state=ready
leased cbx_3960ccf8b93a slug=web provider=local-container ip=127.0.0.1
ready ssh=crabbox@127.0.0.1:61988 network=public

$ cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/crabbox/state/claims/cbx_3960ccf8b93a.json
{ "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "slug":"web", "provider":"local-container",
  "pond":"local-smoke", "repoRoot":"/Users/zozo123/crabbox", ... }

$ crabbox pond peers --pond local-smoke --json
{ "members": [{ "slug":"web", "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "provider":"local-container",
                "pond":"local-smoke", "transport":"pending", "transports":["ssh"],
                "endpoint":"", "note":"ssh endpoint not yet recorded for this lease" }] }

$ crabbox pond connect local-smoke --export        # no --provider flag — cross-provider iteration via capability
pond "local-smoke" SSH-mesh ready (1 forwards)
export CRABBOX_POND_WEB_8080=127.0.0.1:51820
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/hosts
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/env
  -L 127.0.0.1:51820 -> web:8080
```

## Security notes

- `TS_API_KEY` in operator shell empowers `crabbox warmup --pond` to PUT the operator's tailnet ACL policy via the auto-bootstrap path. The mint happens client-side only; the broker never holds Tailscale credentials. Document this explicitly because it's a real elevation introduced by this PR.
- Default within a pond is allow-all between members (one ACL grant per pond). Multi-agent isolation use cases need a future `--isolation per-slug` flag — not in this PR.
- Pond ACL tag scope is `tag:cbx-pond-<base32(sha256(operator-email))[:7]>-<pond-name>` — deterministic hash, no truncation-collision risk between operators with similar email prefixes on shared org tailnets (R4 fix replacing the prior 7-char left-truncate).
- Codex P1 findings on the structural acls/grants parse (`doctor_pond.go`) and the hujson decode (`pond_acl.go`) — both with concrete silent-failure security gaps — are resolved inline; their tests now cover the previously-missing per-rule structural matching and the HuJSON-comment policy shape.

## Compatibility notes

- Additive surface: `--pond` flag, `pond=` reserved label key, `BridgePeer.transports []string` (kept the existing `transport string` for backward compat), new `FeatureURLBridge` const, three new pond commands. Existing single-box flows unchanged. Single-box use is a "pond of one" with no networking mechanics fired.
- Behavior change worth highlighting: `providerTransportClass` is now derived from `Spec().Features` rather than the previous hardcoded switch. Side effect — corrects a pre-existing miscategorization where Proxmox and static SSH were mapped to `TransportTailnet` despite neither declaring `FeatureTailscale`; they now correctly primary to `TransportSSH`.
- Reserved provider-label key `pond` — operators with a pre-existing `pond=…` provider label collide. The label is marked preview-tier (see API stability below).
- API stability: `pond` is **preview** for v0.x. Reserved label key stays; flag shape may evolve. Stable from v1.0.

Closes #136, closes #137.
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: author association NONE and repository permission none are not allowed

## Live smoke — Bridge plane (Islo)

```
$ crabbox warmup --pond ps001 --slug w --provider islo --islo-memory-mb 2048 --islo-disk-gb 10 --ttl 5m
leased isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e slug=w provider=islo sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
warmup complete total=42.17s

$ crabbox pond peers --pond ps001 --json
{"members":[{"slug":"w","leaseID":"isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e","provider":"islo","pond":"ps001","transport":"url","transports":["url"],"endpoint":""}]}

$ crabbox release --provider islo isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
released lease=isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
```

## Live smoke — Tailscale plane (ACL bootstrap end-to-end)

Against a real tailnet with `TS_API_KEY` exported. Read live policy (HuJSON-aware decode — R1 fix), merged the new row into `tagOwners` + `grants`, PUT with `If-Match`.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider hetzner --pond live-smoke --slug node-1 --tailscale --ttl 1m
# (warmup fails on the missing CRABBOX_TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY check; ACL write fires *before* provider provisioning.)

$ curl -s -u $TS_API_KEY: https://api.tailscale.com/api/v2/tailnet/-/acl | jq '.tagOwners, (.grants[] | select(.src[0] | startswith("tag:cbx-pond-")))'
{ "tag:cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha": ["autogroup:admin"],
  "tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke": ["autogroup:admin"] }
{ "src": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "dst": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "ip":  ["*"] }
```

New owner segment is `ipdkyao` — `base32(sha256("yossi-eliaz"))[:7]` (R4 fix) — sitting beside the stale `cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha` row from earlier testing, confirming the hash does not collide. Idempotent re-run of `crabbox doctor --pond live-smoke --provider hetzner` reports `ok pond: Tailscale plane auto-managed (tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke)`. Test row cleaned up: `grep -c cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke` on the live policy = 0.

## Live smoke — SSH-mesh cross-provider (local-container / Docker)

Proves the capability refactor's load-bearing claim: a non-SSH-only provider (`local-container` advertises `FeatureSSH` but is not in the legacy SSH-only class) is now `pond connect`-eligible.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider local-container --pond local-smoke --slug web --expose 8080 --ttl 5m
provisioned lease=cbx_3960ccf8b93a container=aeb808878095 state=ready
leased cbx_3960ccf8b93a slug=web provider=local-container ip=127.0.0.1
ready ssh=crabbox@127.0.0.1:61988 network=public

$ cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/crabbox/state/claims/cbx_3960ccf8b93a.json
{ "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "slug":"web", "provider":"local-container",
  "pond":"local-smoke", "repoRoot":"/Users/zozo123/crabbox", ... }

$ crabbox pond peers --pond local-smoke --json
{ "members": [{ "slug":"web", "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "provider":"local-container",
                "pond":"local-smoke", "transport":"pending", "transports":["ssh"],
                "endpoint":"", "note":"ssh endpoint not yet recorded for this lease" }] }

$ crabbox pond connect local-smoke --export        # no --provider flag — cross-provider iteration via capability
pond "local-smoke" SSH-mesh ready (1 forwards)
export CRABBOX_POND_WEB_8080=127.0.0.1:51820
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/hosts
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/env
  -L 127.0.0.1:51820 -> web:8080
```

## Security notes

- `TS_API_KEY` in operator shell empowers `crabbox warmup --pond` to PUT the operator's tailnet ACL policy via the auto-bootstrap path. The mint happens client-side only; the broker never holds Tailscale credentials. Document this explicitly because it's a real elevation introduced by this PR.
- Default within a pond is allow-all between members (one ACL grant per pond). Multi-agent isolation use cases need a future `--isolation per-slug` flag — not in this PR.
- Pond ACL tag scope is `tag:cbx-pond-<base32(sha256(operator-email))[:7]>-<pond-name>` — deterministic hash, no truncation-collision risk between operators with similar email prefixes on shared org tailnets (R4 fix replacing the prior 7-char left-truncate).
- Codex P1 findings on the structural acls/grants parse (`doctor_pond.go`) and the hujson decode (`pond_acl.go`) — both with concrete silent-failure security gaps — are resolved inline; their tests now cover the previously-missing per-rule structural matching and the HuJSON-comment policy shape.

## Compatibility notes

- Additive surface: `--pond` flag, `pond=` reserved label key, `BridgePeer.transports []string` (kept the existing `transport string` for backward compat), new `FeatureURLBridge` const, three new pond commands. Existing single-box flows unchanged. Single-box use is a "pond of one" with no networking mechanics fired.
- Behavior change worth highlighting: `providerTransportClass` is now derived from `Spec().Features` rather than the previous hardcoded switch. Side effect — corrects a pre-existing miscategorization where Proxmox and static SSH were mapped to `TransportTailnet` despite neither declaring `FeatureTailscale`; they now correctly primary to `TransportSSH`.
- Reserved provider-label key `pond` — operators with a pre-existing `pond=…` provider label collide. The label is marked preview-tier (see API stability below).
- API stability: `pond` is **preview** for v0.x. Reserved label key stays; flag shape may evolve. Stable from v1.0.

Closes #136, closes #137.

target.title: feat: introduce pond (peer discovery + bridge + ssh-mesh)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `--pond NAME` and reserved `pond=` provider label for workflows that need more than one box talking to each other on a private network: multi-service integration tests where compose's loopback hides cross-host bugs (saga / mTLS / CORS / federation timing), CPU + GPU pipelines across providers because no single vendor stocks both at the same price point, cross-repo CI, master/worker compute (BOINC / Dask / Ray cluster shape — not tightly-coupled MPI / NCCL all-reduce, which the cross-vendor RTT can't carry). Single-box flows unchanged ("pond of one").
- Three transport planes, derived from each provider's `Spec().Features` set (no central hardcoded mapping):
  - Tailscale (`FeatureTailscale`) — managed Linux peer mesh via ACL tag + cloud-init systemd timer rewriting `/etc/hosts.cbx`; stable `<slug>.cbx` DNS.
  - Bridge (`FeatureURLBridge`) — Islo / E2B / Railway live; Modal / Cloudflare / Tensorlake stubs return `BridgeState=unsupported` until provider APIs expose per-sandbox ingress.
  - SSH-mesh (`FeatureSSH`) — operator-side `ssh -L` via `crabbox pond connect [--export]`. Works against every provider that advertises FeatureSSH: Hetzner / Azure / GCP / AWS / Proxmox / static SSH / RunPod / exe-dev / Daytona / Sprites / Namespace / Semaphore / local-container / Parallels.
- `BridgePeer.transports[]` reports the full plane set per member; legacy `transport` field still reports the primary (Tailscale > URL > SSH-mesh).
- New verbs: `crabbox pond peers --pond <name>`, `crabbox pond connect <name> [--export]`. `crabbox doctor --pond <name>` reports cross-plane reachability.
- Worker reserves the `pond` label key.
- Renamed `crew` → `pond` (the org uses crab-biology naming; `pool` was taken by `internal/cli/pool.go`).
- Folds #136 (bridge plane) and #137 (ssh-mesh) into this PR.

## Verification

- `go test -race ./internal/cli/...` — 47 tests green, ~41s
- `go vet ./...`
- `( cd worker && pnpm test && pnpm typecheck )`
- `crabbox doctor --pond smoke` against a real Hetzner tailnet

## Live smoke — Bridge plane (Islo)

```
$ crabbox warmup --pond ps001 --slug w --provider islo --islo-memory-mb 2048 --islo-disk-gb 10 --ttl 5m
leased isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e slug=w provider=islo sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
warmup complete total=42.17s

$ crabbox pond peers --pond ps001 --json
{"members":[{"slug":"w","leaseID":"isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e","provider":"islo","pond":"ps001","transport":"url","transports":["url"],"endpoint":""}]}

$ crabbox release --provider islo isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
released lease=isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
```

## Live smoke — Tailscale plane (ACL bootstrap end-to-end)

Against a real tailnet with `TS_API_KEY` exported. Read live policy (HuJSON-aware decode — R1 fix), merged the new row into `tagOwners` + `grants`, PUT with `If-Match`.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider hetzner --pond live-smoke --slug node-1 --tailscale --ttl 1m
# (warmup fails on the missing CRABBOX_TAILSCALE_AUTH_KEY check; ACL write fires *before* provider provisioning.)

$ curl -s -u $TS_API_KEY: https://api.tailscale.com/api/v2/tailnet/-/acl | jq '.tagOwners, (.grants[] | select(.src[0] | startswith("tag:cbx-pond-")))'
{ "tag:cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha": ["autogroup:admin"],
  "tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke": ["autogroup:admin"] }
{ "src": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "dst": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "ip":  ["*"] }
```

New owner segment is `ipdkyao` — `base32(sha256("yossi-eliaz"))[:7]` (R4 fix) — sitting beside the stale `cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha` row from earlier testing, confirming the hash does not collide. Idempotent re-run of `crabbox doctor --pond live-smoke --provider hetzner` reports `ok pond: Tailscale plane auto-managed (tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke)`. Test row cleaned up: `grep -c cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke` on the live policy = 0.

## Live smoke — SSH-mesh cross-provider (local-container / Docker)

Proves the capability refactor's load-bearing claim: a non-SSH-only provider (`local-container` advertises `FeatureSSH` but is not in the legacy SSH-only class) is now `pond connect`-eligible.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider local-container --pond local-smoke --slug web --expose 8080 --ttl 5m
provisioned lease=cbx_3960ccf8b93a container=aeb808878095 state=ready
leased cbx_3960ccf8b93a slug=web provider=local-container ip=127.0.0.1
ready ssh=crabbox@127.0.0.1:61988 network=public

$ cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/crabbox/state/claims/cbx_3960ccf8b93a.json
{ "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "slug":"web", "provider":"local-container",
  "pond":"local-smoke", "repoRoot":"/Users/zozo123/crabbox", ... }

$ crabbox pond peers --pond local-smoke --json
{ "members": [{ "slug":"web", "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "provider":"local-container",
                "pond":"local-smoke", "transport":"pending", "transports":["ssh"],
                "endpoint":"", "note":"ssh endpoint not yet recorded for this lease" }] }

$ crabbox pond connect local-smoke --export        # no --provider flag — cross-provider iteration via capability
pond "local-smoke" SSH-mesh ready (1 forwards)
export CRABBOX_POND_WEB_8080=127.0.0.1:51820
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/hosts
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/env
  -L 127.0.0.1:51820 -> web:8080
```

## Security notes

- `TS_API_KEY` in operator shell empowers `crabbox warmup --pond` to PUT the operator's tailnet ACL policy via the auto-bootstrap path. The mint happens client-side only; the broker never holds Tailscale credentials. Document this explicitly because it's a real elevation introduced by this PR.
- Default within a pond is allow-all between members (one ACL grant per pond). Multi-agent isolation use cases need a future `--isolation per-slug` flag — not in this PR.
- Pond ACL tag scope is `tag:cbx-pond-<base32(sha256(operator-email))[:7]>-<pond-name>` — deterministic hash, no truncation-collision risk between operators with similar email prefixes on shared org tailnets (R4 fix replacing the prior 7-char left-truncate).
- Codex P1 findings on the structural acls/grants parse (`doctor_pond.go`) and the hujson decode (`pond_acl.go`) — both with concrete silent-failure security gaps — are resolved inline; their tests now cover the previously-missing per-rule structural matching and the HuJSON-comment policy shape.

## Compatibility notes

- Additive surface: `--pond` flag, `pond=` reserved label key, `BridgePeer.transports []string` (kept the existing `transport string` for backward compat), new `FeatureURLBridge` const, three new pond commands. Existing single-box flows unchanged. Single-box use is a "pond of one" with no networking mechanics fired.
- Behavior change worth highlighting: `providerTransportClass` is now derived from `Spec().Features` rather than the previous hardcoded switch. Side effect — corrects a pre-existing miscategorization where Proxmox and static SSH were mapped to `TransportTailnet` despite neither declaring `FeatureTailscale`; they now correctly primary to `TransportSSH`.
- Reserved provider-label key `pond` — operators with a pre-existing `pond=…` provider label collide. The label is marked preview-tier (see API stability below).
- API stability: `pond` is **preview** for v0.x. Reserved label key stays; flag shape may evolve. Stable from v1.0.

Closes #136, closes #137.

```
{ "tag:cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha": ["autogroup:admin"],
  "tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke": ["autogroup:admin"] }
{ "src": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "dst": ["tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke"],
  "ip":  ["*"] }
```

New owner segment is `ipdkyao` — `base32(sha256("yossi-eliaz"))[:7]` (R4 fix) — sitting beside the stale `cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha` row from earlier testing, confirming the hash does not collide. Idempotent re-run of `crabbox doctor --pond live-smoke --provider hetzner` reports `ok pond: Tailscale plane auto-managed (tag:cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke)`. Test row cleaned up: `grep -c cbx-pond-ipdkyao-live-smoke` on the live policy = 0.

## Live smoke — SSH-mesh cross-provider (local-container / Docker)

Proves the capability refactor's load-bearing claim: a non-SSH-only provider (`local-container` advertises `FeatureSSH` but is not in the legacy SSH-only class) is now `pond connect`-eligible.

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider local-container --pond local-smoke --slug web --expose 8080 --ttl 5m
provisioned lease=cbx_3960ccf8b93a container=aeb808878095 state=ready
leased cbx_3960ccf8b93a slug=web provider=local-container ip=127.0.0.1
ready ssh=crabbox@127.0.0.1:61988 network=public

$ cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/crabbox/state/claims/cbx_3960ccf8b93a.json
{ "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "slug":"web", "provider":"local-container",
  "pond":"local-smoke", "repoRoot":"/Users/zozo123/crabbox", ... }

$ crabbox pond peers --pond local-smoke --json
{ "members": [{ "slug":"web", "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "provider":"local-container",
                "pond":"local-smoke", "transport":"pending", "transports":["ssh"],
                "endpoint":"", "note":"ssh endpoint not yet recorded for this lease" }] }

$ crabbox pond connect local-smoke --export        # no --provider flag — cross-provider iteration via capability
pond "local-smoke" SSH-mesh ready (1 forwards)
export CRABBOX_POND_WEB_8080=127.0.0.1:51820
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/hosts
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/env
  -L 127.0.0.1:51820 -> web:8080
```

## Security notes

- `TS_API_KEY` in operator shell empowers `crabbox warmup --pond` to PUT the operator's tailnet ACL policy via the auto-bootstrap path. The mint happens client-side only; the broker never holds Tailscale credentials. Document this explicitly because it's a real elevation introduced by this PR.
- Default within a pond is allow-all between members (one ACL grant per pond). Multi-agent isolation use cases need a future `--isolation per-slug` flag — not in this PR.
- Pond ACL tag scope is `tag:cbx-pond-<base32(sha256(operator-email))[:7]>-<pond-name>` — deterministic hash, no truncation-collision risk between operators with similar email prefixes on shared org tailnets (R4 fix replacing the prior 7-char left-truncate).
- Codex P1 findings on the structural acls/grants parse (`doctor_pond.go`) and the hujson decode (`pond_acl.go`) — both with concrete silent-failure security gaps — are resolved inline; their tests now cover the previously-missing per-rule structural matching and the HuJSON-comment policy shape.

## Compatibility notes

- Additive surface: `--pond` flag, `pond=` reserved label key, `BridgePeer.transports []string` (kept the existing `transport string` for backward compat), new `FeatureURLBridge` const, three new pond commands. Existing single-box flows unchanged. Single-box use is a "pond of one" with no networking mechanics fired.
- Behavior change worth highlighting: `providerTransportClass` is now derived from `Spec().Features` rather than the previous hardcoded switch. Side effect — corrects a pre-existing miscategorization where Proxmox and static SSH were mapped to `TransportTailnet` despite neither declaring `FeatureTailscale`; they now correctly primary to `TransportSSH`.
- Reserved provider-label key `pond` — operators with a pre-existing `pond=…` provider label collide. The label is marked preview-tier (see API stability below).
- API stability: `pond` is **preview** for v0.x. Reserved label key stays; flag shape may evolve. Stable from v1.0.

Closes #136, closes #137.
